states.name: Missouri

programs.name: Missouri Property Tax Credit (100% Service-Connected Disabled Veterans)
programs.blurb: Veterans who are 100% disabled as a result of military service may claim Missouri's Property Tax Credit for real estate taxes or rent paid on their home.

programs.name: Missouri Returning Heroes Education Act
programs.blurb: Limits tuition charged by Missouri public institutions of higher education for eligible combat veterans.

programs.name: Veterans Preference in Missouri State Employment
programs.blurb: Veterans, certain family members, and survivors receive preference in the state hiring selection process when similarly qualified to other applicants.

programs.name: Missouri State Veterans Homes
programs.blurb: The Missouri Veterans Commission operates seven long-term skilled-care nursing homes for veterans in Cameron, Cape Girardeau, Mexico, Mount Vernon, St. James, St. Louis, and Warrensburg.

programs.name: Military Retirement Pay Income Tax Deduction
programs.blurb: Military retirement benefits are fully deductible from Missouri state income tax.

states.notes: Missouri's official benefits portal is veteranbenefits.mo.gov. The Missouri Department of Conservation also offers hunting/fishing permit exemptions for certain veterans, and MVC operates state veterans cemeteries.

states.name: Montana

agency.name: Montana Veterans Affairs Division (Department of Military Affairs)

programs.name: Montana Disabled Veterans (MDV) Assistance Program
programs.blurb: Reduces the property tax rate on the primary residence of veterans rated 100% disabled from service-connected causes, and of their unmarried surviving spouses.

programs.name: Montana University System Tuition Waivers for Veterans
programs.blurb: The Montana University System offers multiple tuition waivers for eligible veterans, and MVAD provides a scholarship for recipients of the Purple Heart or a higher decoration.

programs.name: Veterans' Employment Preference
programs.blurb: Veterans, disabled veterans, and certain eligible relatives receive hiring preference for Montana state government positions under the state's Veterans' Employment Preference statute.

programs.name: Montana Veterans Homes
programs.blurb: Montana operates skilled-nursing veterans homes in Butte, Columbia Falls, and Glendive.

programs.name: Hunting and Fishing License Benefits for Veterans
programs.blurb: Montana offers discounted hunting and fishing licenses for combat-injured and disabled veterans and special licenses for Purple Heart and Legion of Valor members.

states.notes: Montana also runs the Montana Military Family Relief Fund (grants for activated Guard/Reserve families) and three state veterans cemeteries (Fort Harrison, Miles City, Missoula).

states.name: Nebraska

programs.name: Nebraska Homestead Exemption for Disabled Veterans
programs.blurb: Provides property tax relief on the homestead of qualifying veterans totally disabled by service-connected causes and certain surviving spouses.

programs.name: Waiver of Tuition Program
programs.blurb: Waives tuition at Nebraska public community colleges, state colleges, and the University of Nebraska for eligible dependents of qualifying veterans, covering one degree, diploma, or certificate per level.

programs.name: Veterans' Preference in Public Employment
programs.blurb: Honorably discharged veterans receive additional points on passing scores in examinations for state, county, and municipal jobs, with extra preference for disabled veterans.

programs.name: Nebraska Veterans' Homes
programs.blurb: NDVA operates four state veterans' homes, located in Bellevue, Kearney, Norfolk, and Scottsbluff.

programs.name: Military Retirement Benefits Exclusion
programs.blurb: For tax years beginning on or after January 1, 2022, military retirees may exclude 100% of military retirement benefits from Nebraska taxable income.

states.notes: Nebraska Game & Parks also offers a no-fee lifetime small-game/fish permit for qualifying disabled veterans and a reduced-fee annual permit for veterans 64+ (outdoornebraska.gov/permits/veterans-and-military-permits/). Starting January 1, 2026, disabled and/or blind veterans can receive a motor vehicle tax and fee exemption for one personal vehicle.
